23 In the thirty-first year of Asa's reign over Judah, Omri became king of Israel. He reigned twelve years, six of them in Tirzah.
24 He bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver. He built a city there. He called it Samaria, after the name of Shemer, who had owned the hill.
25 But Omri did evil before the LORD. He did more evil than all the kings before him.
26 He followed all the ways of Jeroboam son of Nebat and his sins. Jeroboam had made Israel sin. They made the LORD, the God of Israel, angry with their useless idols.
27 The rest of the acts of Omri, his strong deeds, and the powerful things he did, are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
28 Omri died like his fathers and was buried in Samaria. His son Ahab became king in his place.
29 In the thirty-eighth year of Asa's reign over Judah, Ahab son of Omri became king of Israel. He reigned in Samaria for twenty-two years.
30 Ahab son of Omri did evil before the LORD. He did more evil than all the kings before him.
31 It was not enough for him to walk in the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. He even married Jezebel, the daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians. Then he began to serve and worship Baal.
32 First, Ahab set up an altar for Baal in the temple of Baal. He had built this temple in Samaria.
33 Then he set up an Asherah pole. Ahab did more to make the LORD, the God of Israel, angry than all the kings of Israel before him.

34 In Ahab's days, Hiel the man from Bethel rebuilt Jericho. He built its base, losing his firstborn son Abiram. He put up its gates, losing his youngest son Segub. This happened just as the LORD had spoken through Joshua son of Nun.

title: 1 Kings 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Elijah the Tishbite lived in Gilead. He said to Ahab, "As the LORD lives—the God of Israel whom I serve—no dew and no rain will fall in these years, unless I say so!"
2 Then the word of the LORD came to Elijah:
3 "Leave here. Go east. Hide yourself by the Brook of Cherith, east of the Jordan.
4 You will drink from the brook. I have told the ravens to feed you there."
5 So Elijah did what the LORD had told him. He went and lived by the Brook of Cherith, east of the Jordan.
6 The ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning and in the evening. He drank from the brook.
7 Some time later, the brook dried up because no rain had fallen in the land.
8 Then the word of the LORD came to Elijah:
9 "Get up and go to Zarephath in Sidon. Stay there. Look, I have told a widow there to feed you."
10 So Elijah got up and went to Zarephath. At the city gate he saw a widow picking up sticks. Elijah called to her and said, "Please bring me a little water in a cup to drink."
11 As she was going to get the water, he called to her again. He said, "Please bring me a piece of bread too."

12 But she replied, "As the LORD your God lives, I have no bread. I have only a little flour in a jar and a little oil in a jug. Look, I am picking up a few sticks to take home. I will make a meal for me and my son. Then we will eat it and die."
13 "Do not be afraid," Elijah said to her. "Go and do as you have said. But first make me a small cake of bread from what you have. Bring it out to me. Then make some for yourself and your son.
14 For this is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'The jar of flour will not become empty. The jug of oil will not become empty. This will last until the day the LORD sends rain on the land.'"
15 So she went and did what Elijah said. There was food every day for Elijah, for the woman, and for her household.
16 The jar of flour did not become empty. The jug of oil did not become empty. The word of the LORD through Elijah came true.
17 Some time later, the woman's son became ill. His sickness grew worse and worse. At last he stopped breathing.
18 The woman said to Elijah, "O man of God, what have you done to me? Did you come to remind me of my sin and to kill my son?"
19 But Elijah said to her, "Give me your son." He took the boy from her arms. He carried him up to the upper room where he was staying. He laid him on his own bed.
20 Then he cried out to the LORD, "O LORD my God, this widow opened her home to me. Have you also brought trouble on her by killing her son?"
21 Then he lay on top of the boy three times. He cried out to the LORD, "O LORD my God, please let this boy's life come back to him!"
22 The LORD heard Elijah. The boy's life came back to him, and he lived.
23 Then Elijah took the boy. He brought him down from the upper room into the house. He gave him to his mother. "Look, your son is alive," Elijah said.
24 Then the woman said to Elijah, "Now I know that you are a man of God. The word of the LORD from your mouth is true."

title: 1 Kings 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 After a long time with no rain, the word of the LORD came to Elijah. He said, "Go and show yourself to Ahab. I will send rain on the land."
2 So Elijah went to show himself to Ahab. The hunger in Samaria was very bad.
3 Ahab called Obadiah, the leader of the king's house. Obadiah loved and feared the LORD very much.
4 When Jezebel had killed the prophets of the LORD, Obadiah took a hundred prophets. He hid them, fifty in each cave, and gave them food and water.
5 Ahab said to Obadiah, "Go through the land. Look at every spring and valley.
6 We may find grass to keep the horses and mules alive. Then we will not lose our animals." So they split the land. Ahab went one way, and Obadiah went the other way.
7 As Obadiah walked along, Elijah suddenly met him. Obadiah knew him, fell on his face, and said, "Is it really you, my lord Elijah?"
8 "It is I," Elijah said. "Go tell your master, 'Elijah is here!'"
9 But Obadiah said, "What wrong have I done? Why do you hand me over to Ahab to be killed?
10 As the LORD your God lives, my master has searched every nation for you. When they said, 'He is not here,' he made them swear it was true.
11 And now you say, 'Go tell your master that Elijah is here!'
12 When I leave you, the Spirit of the LORD may carry you away. Then Ahab will not find you, and he will kill me. But I, your servant, have feared the LORD since I was young.
13 Did no one tell my lord what I did when Jezebel killed the prophets of the LORD? I hid a hundred prophets, fifty in each cave, and gave them food and water.
14 And now you say, 'Go tell your lord that Elijah is here!' He will kill me!"
15 Then Elijah said, "As the LORD of armies lives, the one I serve, I will show myself to Ahab today."
16 So Obadiah went to tell Ahab, and Ahab went to meet Elijah.
17 When Ahab saw Elijah, he said, "Is that you, the man who brings trouble to Israel?"
18 Elijah answered, "I have not brought trouble to Israel. You and your father's house have. You have turned away from the LORD's commands and followed Baal.
19 Now call all Israel to meet me on Mount Carmel. Bring the 450 prophets of Baal and the 400 prophets of Asherah who eat at Jezebel's table."
20 So Ahab called all the Israelites and gathered the prophets on Mount Carmel.
21 Then Elijah came near to all the people and said, "**How long will you waver between two opinions? If the LORD is God, follow him; but if Baal, follow him.**" But the people said nothing.
22 Then Elijah said to the people, "I am the only prophet of the LORD left. But Baal has 450 prophets.

23 Bring us two bulls. Let the prophets of Baal choose one bull. Let them cut it up and place it on the wood, but not light the fire. I will prepare the other bull and place it on the wood, but not light the fire.
24 Then you call on the name of your god. I will call on the name of the LORD. The God who answers with fire — he is God." And all the people said, "That is good."
25 Then Elijah said to the prophets of Baal, "There are many of you. Choose one bull and prepare it first. Then call on the name of your god, but do not light the fire."

26 So they took the bull, prepared it, and called on the name of Baal from morning until noon. They shouted, "O Baal, answer us!" But there was no sound. No one answered. They jumped around the altar they had made.
27 At noon Elijah began to mock them. He said, "Shout louder! He is a god! Maybe he is thinking deeply, or busy, or on a trip. Maybe he is asleep and must be woken up!"
28 So they shouted louder. They cut themselves with knives and spears, as was their way, until blood poured down on them.
29 Noon passed, and they kept shouting until the evening offering time. But there was no answer. No one spoke. No one paid attention.
30 Then Elijah said to all the people, "Come near to me." So all the people came near. He fixed the altar of the LORD that had been broken down.
31 Elijah took twelve stones, one for each tribe of the sons of Jacob. The word of the LORD had come to Jacob and said, "Israel will be your name."
32 With the stones, Elijah built an altar in the name of the LORD. Then he dug a large ditch around the altar.
33 Next, he set the wood in place. He cut up the bull and placed it on the wood.

34 He said, "Fill four big jars with water. Pour the water on the offering and on the wood." Then he said, "Do it again." And they did it again. Then he said, "Do it a third time." And they did it a third time.
35 So the water ran down around the altar. It even filled the ditch.

36 At the evening offering time, Elijah the prophet came near the altar. He said, "O LORD, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, let everyone know today that you are God in Israel. Let them know I am your servant and have done all this at your command.
37 Answer me, O LORD! Answer me! Then these people will know that you, the LORD, are God. And you have turned their hearts back to you."
38 Then the fire of the LORD fell down. It burned up the offering, the wood, the stones, and the dust. It even dried up the water in the ditch.
39 When all the people saw this, they fell on their faces. They said, "**The LORD — he is God! The LORD — he is God!**"
40 Then Elijah said to them, "Catch the prophets of Baal! Do not let one escape." So they caught them. Elijah brought them down to the Kishon Valley and killed them there.
41 Then Elijah said to Ahab, "Go up. Eat and drink. I hear the sound of heavy rain coming."
42 So Ahab went up to eat and drink. But Elijah climbed to the top of Carmel. He bent down on the ground and put his face between his knees.
43 He said to his servant, "Go and look toward the sea." So the servant went and looked. He said, "There is nothing there." Seven times Elijah said, "Go back."
44 The seventh time, the servant said, "**A cloud as small as a man's hand is rising from the sea.**" Elijah said, "Go tell Ahab, 'Get your chariot ready. Go down before the rain stops you.'"
45 Soon the sky grew dark with clouds and wind. A heavy rain began to fall. So Ahab rode away to Jezreel.
46 The power of the LORD came on Elijah. He pulled up his coat at his belt. He ran ahead of Ahab all the way to Jezreel.

title: 1 Kings 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Ahab told Jezebel everything Elijah had done. He told her how Elijah had killed all the prophets with a sword.
2 So Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah. She said, "May the gods punish me hard if I do not kill you by this time tomorrow. I will make your life like the lives of those you killed."
3 Elijah was afraid and ran away to save his life. He came to Beersheba in Judah and left his servant there.
4 He traveled one more day into the desert. He sat down under a tree and prayed that he might die. "I have had too much, LORD," he said. "Take my life. I am no better than my fathers."
5 Then he lay down under the tree and fell asleep. Suddenly an angel touched him and said, "Get up and eat."
6 He looked around. By his head was bread baked over hot coals, and a jar of water. So he ate and drank, and lay down again.
7 A second time the angel of the LORD came back and touched him. The angel said, "Get up and eat, or the journey will be too hard for you."
8 So he got up and ate and drank. That food gave him strength. He walked **40 days and 40 nights** until he reached **Horeb**, the mountain of God.
9 Elijah went into a cave there and spent the night. Then the word of the LORD came to him. The LORD said, "What are you doing here, Elijah?"

10 "I have worked hard for the LORD, the God of Hosts," Elijah said. "But the people of Israel have turned away from Your covenant. They have broken down Your altars. They have killed Your prophets with the sword. I am the only one left, and they want to kill me too."

11 Then the LORD said, "Go out and stand on the mountain before the LORD. Look, the LORD is about to pass by." A very strong wind broke the mountains and the rocks apart before the LORD. But the LORD was not in the wind. After the wind came an earthquake. But the LORD was not in the earthquake.
12 After the earthquake came a fire. But the LORD was not in the fire. And after the fire came **a gentle whisper**.
13 When Elijah heard it, he covered his face with his coat. He went out and stood at the mouth of the cave. Then a voice came to him and said, "What are you doing here, Elijah?"

14 "I have worked hard for the LORD, the God of Hosts," he said. "But the people of Israel have turned away from Your covenant. They have broken down Your altars. They have killed Your prophets with the sword. I am the only one left, and they want to kill me too."
15 Then the LORD said to him, "Go back the way you came. Go to the Desert near Damascus. When you arrive, anoint Hazael as king over Aram.
16 Also anoint Jehu son of Nimshi as king over Israel. And anoint **Elisha** son of Shaphat from Abel-meholah to take your place as prophet.
17 Jehu will kill anyone who escapes the sword of Hazael. And Elisha will kill anyone who escapes the sword of Jehu.
18 But I have kept **7,000 people** in Israel. They have not bowed down to Baal. Their mouths have not kissed him."
19 So Elijah left and found **Elisha** son of Shaphat. Elisha was plowing a field with twelve pairs of oxen. He was with the twelfth pair. Elijah walked by him and put his coat on him.
20 So Elisha left the oxen and ran after Elijah. He said, "Please let me kiss my father and mother goodbye. Then I will follow you." "Go on back," Elijah said. "What have I done to you?"

21 So Elisha turned back from him. He took his pair of oxen and killed them. He used the oxen's wood tools to cook the meat. He gave it to the people, and they ate. Then he started to follow Elijah and serve him.

title: 1 Kings 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Ben-hadad king of Aram gathered all his army. Thirty-two kings with horses and chariots joined him. He marched up and surrounded Samaria, and he attacked it.
2 Then he sent messengers into the city to Ahab king of Israel.
3 They said, "This is what Ben-hadad says: 'Your silver and gold are mine. Your best wives and children are mine!'"
4 The king of Israel replied, "It will be as you say, my lord the king. I am yours, and all that I have is yours."
5 The messengers came back and said, "This is what Ben-hadad says: 'I sent for your silver, your gold, your wives, and your children.
6 But about this time tomorrow, I will send my servants. They will search your palace and your servants' houses. They will take away all that is precious to you.'"
7 Then the king of Israel called all the elders of the land. He said, "See how this man is looking for trouble. He asked for my wives, my children, my silver, and my gold, and I did not say no."
8 The elders and the people all said, "Do not listen to him. Do not agree to what he asks."
9 So Ahab answered Ben-hadad's messengers, "Tell my lord the king, 'I will do all you asked the first time. But this thing I cannot do.'" So the messengers left and told Ben-hadad.
10 Then Ben-hadad sent another message to Ahab: "May the gods punish me hard if enough dust is left in Samaria to give each of my men a handful."
11 The king of Israel replied, "Tell him: 'A soldier putting on his armor should not boast like one who has finished the fight.'"
12 Ben-hadad got this message while he and the kings were drinking in their tents. He said to his servants, "Get ready to attack." So they took their places against the city.
13 Then a prophet came to Ahab king of Israel. He said, "This is what the LORD says: 'Do you see this huge army? Look, I will give it into your hand today. Then you will know that I am the LORD.'"
14 "By whom?" Ahab asked. The prophet replied, "This is what the LORD says: 'By the young soldiers of the local rulers.'" "Who will start the battle?" Ahab asked. "You will," answered the prophet.
15 So Ahab gathered the young soldiers of the local rulers. There were 232 men. Then he gathered the rest of the Israelite army, 7,000 in all.
16 They marched out at noon. Ben-hadad and the 32 kings with him were getting drunk in their tents.
17 The young soldiers of the local rulers marched out first. Ben-hadad had sent out scouts, and they reported, "Men are marching out of Samaria."
18 "If they come in peace, take them alive," he said. "If they come for war, take them alive too."
19 The young soldiers of the local rulers marched out of the city, with the army behind them.
20 Each man struck down his enemy. So the Arameans ran away, and the Israelites chased them. But Ben-hadad king of Aram escaped on a horse with his horsemen.
21 Then the king of Israel marched out and attacked the horses and chariots. He killed many of the Arameans.
22 After this, the prophet came to the king of Israel. He said, "Go and make your army strong. Think well about what you must do. In the spring the king of Aram will come up against you again."
23 The servants of the king of Aram said to him, "Their gods are gods of the hills. That is why they beat us. Let us fight them on the plains. Then we will surely beat them.
24 So do this: take away all the kings from their places. Put other officers in their places.
25 Then raise an army like the one you lost — horse for horse and chariot for chariot. We will fight the Israelites on the plain, and we will surely beat them." The king liked their plan and did it.
26 In the spring, Ben-hadad gathered the Arameans and went up to Aphek to fight Israel.
27 The Israelites also got ready, gathered supplies, and marched out to meet them. They camped before the Arameans like two small groups of goats. But the Arameans filled the open land.

28 Then the man of God came to the king of Israel. He said, "This is what the LORD says: 'The Arameans think the LORD is a god of the hills and not of the valleys. So I will give this huge army into your hand. Then you will know that I am the LORD.'"
29 For seven days the two armies camped facing each other. On the seventh day the battle began. The Israelites struck down the Arameans — a hundred thousand foot soldiers in one day.
30 The rest ran into the city of Aphek. There the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand of them. Ben-hadad also ran to the city and hid in an inner room.

31 Then Ben-hadad's servants said to him, "Look, we have heard that the kings of Israel are kind. Let us go to the king of Israel with rough cloth on our bodies and ropes on our heads. Maybe he will let you live."

32 So they put rough cloth on their bodies and ropes on their heads. They went to the king of Israel and said, "Your servant Ben-hadad says, 'Please let me live.'" The king answered, "Is he still alive? He is my brother."

33 The men were looking for a sign of hope. They quickly took his word and said, "Yes, your brother Ben-hadad." "Go and bring him!" said the king. Then Ben-hadad came out, and Ahab let him come up into his chariot.

34 Ben-hadad said to him, "I will give back the cities my father took from your father. You may set up your own markets in Damascus, as my father did in Samaria." Ahab replied, "I will let you go with this peace deal." So he made a peace deal with him and sent him away.
35 By the word of the LORD, one of the prophet's group said to his friend, "Please strike me!" But the man refused to strike him.
36 Then the prophet said to him, "You have not obeyed the voice of the LORD. So as soon as you leave me, a lion will kill you." When he left, a lion found him and killed him.
37 Then the prophet found another man and said, "Please strike me!" So the man struck him and wounded him.
38 Then the prophet went and waited on the road for the king. He hid his face with a cloth over his eyes.

39 When the king passed by, the prophet cried out to him: "Your servant went out into the middle of the battle. Suddenly a man came over with a prisoner. He said, 'Guard this man! If he is lost, your life will pay for his life. Or you must pay a big weight of silver.'
40 But while your servant was busy here and there, the man got away." The king of Israel said to him, "That will be your judgment. You have said it yourself."
41 Then the prophet quickly took the cloth off his eyes. The king of Israel knew him as one of the prophets.
42 The prophet said to the king, "This is what the LORD says: 'You let go the man I had marked for death. So your life will pay for his life, and your people for his people.'"
43 Sad and angry, the king of Israel went home to Samaria.

title: 1 Kings 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Some time later, Naboth from Jezreel had a vineyard. It was in Jezreel next to King Ahab's palace in Samaria.
2 Ahab said to Naboth, "Give me your vineyard. I want a vegetable garden next to my palace. I will give you a better vineyard. Or I can pay you in silver."
3 But Naboth replied, "May the LORD stop me. I cannot give you my fathers' land."
4 So Ahab went home sad and angry. Naboth had said, "I will not give you the land my fathers gave me." Ahab lay on his bed, turned away, and would not eat.
5 Soon his wife Jezebel came in. She asked, "Why are you so sad? Why will you not eat?"
6 Ahab answered, "I spoke to Naboth from Jezreel. I said, 'Sell me your vineyard for silver.' I also said, 'Or I will give you another one.' But he said, 'I will not give you my vineyard!'"
7 His wife Jezebel said to him, "Are you not the king of Israel? Get up and eat. Be happy! I will get you Naboth's vineyard."
8 Then Jezebel wrote letters in Ahab's name. She closed them with his seal. She sent them to the elders and leaders in Naboth's city.
9 In the letters she wrote: "Call for a fast day. Give Naboth an honored seat among the people.
10 But put two evil men in front of him. Have them say, 'You have cursed God and the king!' Then take him out of the city. Throw stones at him until he dies."
11 So the elders and leaders in Naboth's city did what Jezebel said. They followed her letters.
12 They called for a fast day. They gave Naboth an honored seat among the people.

13 Then the two evil men came in and sat in front of Naboth. They spoke against him before the people. They said, "Naboth has cursed God and the king!" So they took him out of the city. They threw stones at him until he died.
14 Then they sent word to Jezebel. They said, "Naboth has been stoned to death."
15 When Jezebel heard that Naboth was dead, she spoke to Ahab. She said, "Get up and take the vineyard of Naboth from Jezreel. He would not sell it to you. But Naboth is no longer alive."
16 When Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, he got up. He went down to take Naboth's vineyard.
17 Then the LORD spoke to Elijah the Tishbite. He said,
18 "Get up and go to meet Ahab king of Israel. He is in Samaria. Look, he is in Naboth's vineyard. He has gone to take it.
19 Tell Ahab this is what the LORD says: 'Did you not kill a man? Did you not take his land?' Then say: 'The dogs licked up Naboth's blood. In that same place, the dogs will lick your blood too!'"
20 When Elijah arrived, Ahab said to him, "So you have found me, my enemy." Elijah replied, "I have found you. You gave yourself to do evil before the LORD.
21 This is what the LORD says: 'I will bring trouble on you. I will destroy your family. I will remove from Ahab every man in Israel, both servants and free men.
22 I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat. I will make it like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah. You made Me angry. You led Israel into sin.'
23 The LORD also speaks about Jezebel. He says: 'The dogs will eat Jezebel by the wall of Jezreel.'
24 Anyone in Ahab's house who dies in the city, the dogs will eat. Anyone who dies in the field, the birds of the sky will eat."
25 There was never anyone like Ahab. He gave himself to do evil before the LORD. His wife Jezebel pushed him to do it.
26 He did very bad things. He followed idols, just like the Amorites. The LORD had driven the Amorites out before the Israelites.
27 When Ahab heard these words, he tore his clothes. He put on sackcloth and stopped eating. He lay in sackcloth. He walked around in a humble way.
28 Then the LORD spoke to Elijah the Tishbite. He said,
29 "Have you seen how Ahab has made himself low before Me? Because he has made himself low, I will not bring the trouble in his days. But I will bring it on his house in the days of his son."

title: 1 Kings 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Kings
paragraphs: 1 For three years there was no war between Aram and Israel.
2 In the third year, Jehoshaphat king of Judah went to visit the king of Israel.
3 The king of Israel said to his servants, "Ramoth-gilead belongs to us.

4 But we have not taken it back from the king of Aram." Then he asked Jehoshaphat, "Will you go with me to fight against Ramoth-gilead?" Jehoshaphat answered, "I am like you. My people are your people. My horses are your horses."
5 But Jehoshaphat also said, "Please ask the LORD first for his word."

6 So the king of Israel called the prophets together — about four hundred men. He asked them, "Should I go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or not?" They said, "Go up. The Lord will give it into the king's hand."
7 But Jehoshaphat asked, "Is there no other prophet of the LORD here to ask?"

8 The king of Israel answered, "There is still one man who can ask the LORD. But I hate him. He never speaks good news about me, only bad. He is Micaiah son of Imlah." Jehoshaphat replied, "The king should not say that!"
9 So the king of Israel called one of his officials and said, "Bring Micaiah son of Imlah quickly."
10 The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah wore their royal robes. They sat on their thrones at the threshing floor by the gate of Samaria. All the prophets spoke before them.
11 Zedekiah son of Chenaanah had made iron horns for himself. He said, "This is what the LORD says: 'With these you will push the Arameans until they are destroyed.'"
12 All the prophets said the same thing. "Go up to Ramoth-gilead and win. The LORD will give it into the king's hand."
13 The messenger who went to call Micaiah said to him, "Look, all the prophets speak good news to the king. Please let your words agree with theirs. Speak good news too."
14 But Micaiah said, "As surely as the LORD lives, I will speak only what the LORD tells me."
15 When Micaiah came, the king asked him, "Micaiah, should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or not?" Micaiah answered, "Go up and win. The LORD will give it into the king's hand."
16 But the king said to him, "How many times must I make you swear to tell me only the truth in the name of the LORD?"
17 So Micaiah said, "I saw all Israel scattered on the hills like sheep without a shepherd. And the LORD said, 'These people have no master. Let each one go home in peace.'"
18 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Did I not tell you? He never speaks good news about me, only bad."
19 Micaiah went on, "Therefore hear the word of the LORD. I saw the LORD sitting on his throne. All the host of heaven stood by him on his right and on his left.
20 The LORD said, 'Who will trick Ahab into going to fall at Ramoth-gilead?' One said this, and another said that.
21 Then a spirit came forward and stood before the LORD. He said, 'I will trick him.' The LORD asked, 'How?'
22 He replied, 'I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.' The LORD said, 'You will trick him and win. Go and do it.'
23 So you see, the LORD has put a lying spirit in the mouths of all these prophets of yours. The LORD has spoken disaster against you."
24 Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah came up and hit Micaiah in the face. He asked, "Which way did the Spirit of the LORD go when he left me to speak with you?"
25 Micaiah replied, "You will soon see — on the day you go and hide in an inner room."
26 The king of Israel said, "Take Micaiah back to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king's son.
27 Tell them this is what the king says: 'Put this man in prison. Feed him only bread and water until I return safely.'"
28 But Micaiah said, "If you ever return safely, the LORD has not spoken through me." Then he added, "Listen, all you people!"
29 So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
30 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "I will hide my face and go into battle. But you wear your royal robes." So the king of Israel hid his face and went into battle.
31 The king of Aram had ordered his thirty-two chariot commanders, "Do not fight anyone, small or great, except the king of Israel."
32 When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they said, "Surely this is the king of Israel!" So they turned to fight him. But Jehoshaphat cried out.
33 When the chariot commanders saw he was not the king of Israel, they turned back.

34 But a man pulled his bow without aiming. He struck the king of Israel between the joints of his armor. The king said to his driver, "Turn around. Take me out of the battle, for I am badly wounded!"
35 The battle raged all day. The king was held up in his chariot facing the Arameans. Blood from his wound ran onto the floor of the chariot. That evening he died.
36 As the sun was setting, a cry went through the army. "Every man to his own city! Every man to his own land!"
37 So the king died, and they brought him to Samaria. They buried him there.
38 They washed the chariot at the pool of Samaria where the prostitutes bathed. The dogs licked up Ahab's blood, just as the LORD had said.
39 As for the other things Ahab did, and all that he built — including the ivory palace and the cities — they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
40 Ahab rested with his fathers. His son Ahaziah became king after him.
41 In the fourth year of Ahab's reign over Israel, Jehoshaphat son of Asa became king of Judah.
42 Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he became king. He reigned in Jerusalem twenty-five years. His mother's name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.

43 Jehoshaphat walked in all the ways of his father Asa. He did not turn away from them. He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD. But the high places were not removed. The people still offered sacrifices and burned incense on the high places.
44 Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
45 As for the other things Jehoshaphat did, his strength, and his wars — they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah.
46 He drove out of the land the male shrine prostitutes who remained from the days of his father Asa.
47 There was no king in Edom at that time. A deputy served as king.
48 Jehoshaphat built ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold. But they never set sail, because they were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
49 At that time Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat, "Let my servants sail with your servants." But Jehoshaphat refused.
50 Jehoshaphat rested with his fathers. They buried him with them in the city of his father David. His son Jehoram became king after him.
51 In the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat's reign over Judah, Ahaziah son of Ahab became king of Israel. He reigned in Samaria two years.
52 He did evil in the sight of the LORD. He walked in the ways of his father and mother and of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who had caused Israel to sin.
53 Ahaziah served and worshiped Baal. He made the LORD, the God of Israel, angry, just as his father had done. **Notes:** End of 1 Kings. Pickup point: 2 Kings 1 — Ahaziah and Elijah.
